skill.name: daily.dev
skill.description: Overcome LLM knowledge cutoffs with real-time developer content. daily.dev aggregates articles from thousands of sources, validated by community engagement, with structured taxonomy for precise discovery.
skill.summary: Access real-time developer articles and trending topics through the daily.dev API to overcome AI knowledge cutoffs.
skill.author: dailydotdev

security_audit.summary: All 105 static analysis findings are false positives from markdown code examples in documentation. The skill file contains only documentation with shell command examples for secure token storage. No executable code, command injection vulnerabilities, or actual security risks exist. The skill safely provides API reference information for agents to make authenticated requests to daily.dev.

low_findings.title: API Token Handling Requires User Discipline
low_findings.description: The skill documents proper token security practices but relies on users to follow them. Users must protect their daily.dev API tokens and never expose them.

content.user_title: Access Real-Time Developer News with daily.dev API
content.value_statement: LLM knowledge cutoffs limit access to current developer content. daily.dev provides real-time articles from thousands of sources with community validation and structured taxonomy for precise discovery.
actual_capabilities: Fetch personalized developer feeds from thousands of sources
Search articles by tags, topics, or keywords with structured taxonomy
Create custom feeds filtered to specific technologies or interests
Retrieve trending and popular posts with community engagement metrics
Access full OpenAPI specification for all available endpoints
Manage bookmarks and reading lists for research workflows
limitations: Requires daily.dev Plus subscription and API token to function
Rate limited to 60 requests per minute per user
Requires Bash tool to execute curl commands for API requests
Token storage and security are user-managed (skill provides guidance only)

use_cases.title: Build Personalized Tech News Feed
use_cases.description: Scan GitHub repositories to detect your tech stack, then automatically create a personalized daily.dev feed with relevant articles about your dependencies and frameworks.
use_cases.target_user: Developers who want curated content based on their actual projects

use_cases.title: Stay Current on Technology Trends
use_cases.description: Monitor trending articles and popular discussions to stay ahead of emerging technologies, security updates, and paradigm shifts in your tech stack.
use_cases.target_user: Technical leads and decision-makers tracking industry developments

use_cases.title: Research and Learning Assistant
use_cases.description: When learning new technologies, create custom feeds and bookmark lists to track progress from beginner to advanced content with diverse perspectives.
use_cases.target_user: Developers expanding their skills or onboarding to new stacks

prompt_templates.title: Basic Feed Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search daily.dev for articles about {topic} and summarize the top 5 most relevant posts with their key points.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick overview of current discussions on a specific technology

prompt_templates.title: Tech Stack Personalization
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the package.json and other config files in my current project, then create a personalized daily.dev feed based on the detected technologies.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up tailored news feed based on actual project dependencies

prompt_templates.title: Trending Topics Analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the trending posts from daily.dev and identify which technologies in my stack have recent updates, security issues, or major releases.
prompt_templates.scenario: Proactive monitoring of changes in technologies you actively use

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Perspective Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search daily.dev for coverage of {topic} from at least 3 different sources, then synthesize the perspectives into a balanced comparison with citations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deep research requiring diverse viewpoints and authoritative sources

output_examples.input: What's trending in React development this week?
output_examples.output: Based on daily.dev trending feeds, here are the key developments in React this week: 1) React 19 RC discussions focus on new compiler features, 2) Server Components adoption patterns are emerging with case studies, 3) State management libraries are releasing React 19-compatible versions. The community is actively debating migration strategies...

output_examples.input: Create a feed for my Node.js project
output_examples.output: I've analyzed your package.json and detected Node.js, Express, and PostgreSQL. I've created a custom daily.dev feed following tags: nodejs, expressjs, postgresql, backend-development. Your personalized feed will surface articles relevant to your stack. You can now fetch this feed with: curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $DAILY_DEV_TOKEN" https://api.daily.dev/public/v1/feeds/custom/{feedId}

best_practices: Store your daily.dev API token in environment variables or OS secure storage, never commit it to code repositories
Check rate limit headers in API responses to avoid hitting the 60 requests/minute limit
Use structured tag filtering via /tags endpoint to ensure precise content matching for your tech stack
Leverage custom feeds and bookmark lists to organize research workflows and track learning progress
anti_patterns: Hardcoding API tokens directly in skill files or sharing them in conversations
Making API requests without checking for existing DAILY_DEV_TOKEN environment variable first
Ignoring rate limit headers and causing 429 errors that interrupt workflows
Fetching generic feeds instead of creating targeted custom feeds for specific research needs

faq.question: Do I need a daily.dev subscription to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, you need a daily.dev Plus subscription to access the API. You can subscribe at app.daily.dev/plus and create your API token in the settings.

faq.question: How do I keep my API token secure?
faq.answer: Store your token in environment variables or OS secure storage (macOS Keychain, Windows Credential Manager, Linux Secret Service). Never commit tokens to code or share them publicly.

faq.question: What can I do if I hit the rate limit?
faq.answer: The API allows 60 requests per minute. Check the X-RateLimit-Reset header to see when the window resets. Implement caching strategies to minimize redundant requests.

faq.question: Can this skill help me learn a new technology?
faq.answer: Yes. Create custom feeds filtered to your target technology, build bookmark lists to track progress, and use the search endpoints to find diverse perspectives and tutorials.

faq.question: How does this overcome LLM knowledge cutoffs?
faq.answer: daily.dev aggregates real-time articles from thousands of developer sources. Agents can fetch current information to supplement their training data and provide up-to-date advice.

faq.question: What endpoints are available?
faq.answer: The full OpenAPI specification is at api.daily.dev/public/v1/docs/json. Key endpoints include feeds (foryou, popular, custom), search (posts, sources, tags), and bookmark management.
